Closes the 8-drain saga: Sprint 21 Round 1 (US-252 tick decoupled from display), Sprint 22 Round 2 (US-262 forensic logger + US-265/266/267 discriminator-trio), Sprint 23 Round 3 (US-275 INFO log + US-276 state-file writer + US-277 deploy auto-wire + US-278 doc), Sprint 24 the actual fix (US-279 event-driven callback). Plus opportunistic US-282 Ralph-commit-discipline detector + US-283 startup_log schema audit.

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-23 close baseline. main@cd8088c (V0.23.0). Sprint 23 +15 tests (US-274 sprint_lint filestotouch + US-275/276/277 NEW test files via rescue commit 6d8af99). TD-040 closure (US-272) eliminated the long-standing seed-version test red. Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Drain Test 8 (2026-05-03 08:50-09:08 CDT) ran with full Sprint 23 instrumentation and ISOLATED THE BUG: PowerDownOrchestrator.tick() reads power_source from a stale/decoupled view. UpsMonitor correctly logs BATTERY transition at 08:50:22 but orchestrator never sees it. All 214 tick decisions logged reason=power_source!=BATTERY across 17.5-min drain. Zero _enterStage calls, zero STAGE_* rows in power_log, hard crash at VCELL ~3.30V (buck-converter dropout knee, normal hardware). H1 (tick liveness) ELIMINATED Sprint 23. H2 (gating logic) CONFIRMED + LOCATED Sprint 24. H3 (write path) UNTESTABLE until H2 fixed. CIO Pi5 brownout DISPROVEN (throttled_hex 0x0 entire drain). Sprint 24 ships the fix.

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): All 5 stories TIER 1 (no inter-story deps). Recommended: US-279 first (lead P0, the actual ladder fix; gates Drain Test 9 verdict), US-280 in parallel (silent-fail diagnosis on US-276), then US-281 anti-pattern doc + US-283 startup_log audit + US-282 lint extension can run in any order.

THEME: 'The fix.' Sprint 21-23 instrumentation pinned the bug; Sprint 24 fixes it. Per Spool's note: do not let this be the sprint where Ralph fixes-and-prays. Test discipline is the gate -- integration test must FAIL pre-fix and PASS post-fix per specs/methodology.md 'Integration Tests for Runtime-Verifiable Bugs' subsection (Sprint 21 US-256 pattern).

CONSTRAINTS: avoid Sprint 23 mistakes. (a) Per BL-009 lesson: NO architectural ambiguity in story contracts -- US-279 mandates Option B (event-driven callback) explicitly per CIO 2026-05-03 approval; Ralph does NOT choose between A/B/C. (b) Per US-272/273 spec-divergence pattern: every UPDATE path verified on disk before grooming (audited 2026-05-03); zero phantom paths. (c) Per US-262/275/276/277 commit-but-not-stage bug (rescued via 096dade Sprint 22 + 6d8af99 Sprint 23): US-282 ships the detector to prevent recurrence. (d) Per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md: every fix story has a synthetic test that would FAIL pre-fix.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-03 sprint24-ladder-fix-bug-isolated inbox note (Drain 8 forensic summary + bug location + 3 fix options + acceptance) + Drain 8 forensic CSV at /var/log/eclipse-obd/drain-forensics-20260503T135023Z.csv on Pi (177 data rows, 100% reason=power_source!=BATTERY) + 8-drain test history (1-5 pre-instrumentation, 6 post-US-252, 7 forensic-logger, 8 full-Sprint-23) + offices/pm/action-items.md AI-002 candidate.

POST-SPRINT-24 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO + Spool run Drain Test 9 with US-279 deployed; expect STAGE_WARNING / STAGE_IMMINENT / STAGE_TRIGGER rows in power_log with timestamps matching VCELL crossings + clean shutdown record + auto-boot on wall-power return. (2) Spool reads power_log + drain-forensics CSV post-Drain-9 + writes inbox note declaring 8-drain saga CLOSED. (3) Marcus bumps deploy/RELEASE_VERSION V0.23.0 -> V0.24.0 at sprint close (MINOR for the ladder fix). (4) Stale local sprint branches cleanup (10 now). (5) Spool may file Sprint 25 spec for any follow-on observations from Drain 9 (e.g., timing tuning, hysteresis adjustments). Sprint 24 closes when synthetic + integration tests pass.

stories.title: Ladder fix: UpsMonitor event-driven callback to orchestrator
stories.intent: Drain Test 8 isolated the 8-drain bug definitively: PowerDownOrchestrator reads power_source from a stale/decoupled view. UpsMonitor correctly logged BATTERY transition at 08:50:22 but orchestrator's tick() saw not-BATTERY for all 214 ticks across 17.5-min drain. Cause class: orchestrator never queries UpsMonitor's live state on each tick + UpsMonitor never notifies orchestrator. Per CIO 2026-05-03 approval (BL-009 lesson -- no architectural ambiguity in contracts): Sprint 24 mandates Option B (event-driven callback). UpsMonitor exposes a register-callback API; orchestrator registers itself at lifecycle wiring time; on every PowerSource transition UpsMonitor invokes all registered callbacks with the new source. Orchestrator updates self._powerSource synchronously in the callback; subsequent tick() reads the fresh value and the gating guard passes when on BATTERY.

src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (UPDATE -- replace stale-or-decoupled power_source read in tick() with a self._powerSource attribute updated by an _onPowerSourceChange(newSource) callback method; remove any stale cached read pattern; document the new contract in mod history)

stories.title: Diagnose + fix US-276 state-file writer silent-fail at runtime
stories.intent: Sprint 23 US-276 shipped a state-file writer in PowerDownOrchestrator (writes /var/run/eclipse-obd/orchestrator-state.json on every tick via os.replace atomic rename). Code is in place per post-deploy verification. US-277 deploy step does create /var/run/eclipse-obd/ owned mcornelison:mcornelison. BUT Drain Test 8 CSV proves the file is NOT being written at runtime: pd_stage=unknown and pd_tick_count=-1 on all 177 data rows. Symptom = silent failure. Cause class candidates: (a) PermissionError caught by broad except + not loud-logged; (b) eclipse-obd.service runs as a different user than the dir owner; (c) writer's code path is wrapped in try/except that swallows the error class; (d) writer's path or atomic-rename pattern fails on the deployed Pi filesystem. This story diagnoses (read deployed Pi state + find error swallow point) + fixes (replace silent except with loud ERROR log + first-failure alarm event; subsequent failures suppressed to avoid log spam).

filesToTouch: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (UPDATE -- in _writeStateFile error handling: replace any silent except / broad-swallow with logger.error capturing exception type + message + path; track first-failure flag so subsequent failures suppressed; raise an alarm event ONCE on first failure so post-mortem can tell from journalctl whether the writer ever succeeded; preserve graceful no-crash invariant for tick loop)

tests/pi/power/test_orchestrator_state_file.py (UPDATE -- add tests covering the runtime failure modes: (1) PermissionError on os.replace -> assert ERROR log + first-failure alarm + tick continues; (2) FileNotFoundError on parent dir -> same; (3) silent-success after permanent failure -> assert no duplicate alarms; runtime-validation gate: the new tests would FAIL pre-fix because the existing except swallowed silently)

stories.title: Stale-state guard anti-pattern doc
stories.intent: The 8-drain bug class (cross-component state shared by reference, not by getter or callback, with no freshness contract) deserves an explicit anti-pattern doc so the next instance gets caught at code-review time rather than 8 drains later. Per Spool Sprint 24 Story 3: document the bug class as a project anti-pattern, with the 3 escape hatches enumerated (TTL freshness contract; explicit pull semantics via getter; push-with-acknowledgment via callback). Reference the 8-drain saga as the cautionary tale + cross-link to US-279's Option B fix as the canonical example of escape-hatch #3.

stories.title: AI-002 close: sprint_lint commit-vs-claim verifier
stories.intent: TWO occurrences in two sprints of Ralph's commit-but-not-stage bug: Sprint 22 US-262 (rescue commit 096dade) + Sprint 23 US-275/276/277 (rescue commit 6d8af99). Pattern: Ralph's per-story feat commits LOG the work in commit messages + populate sprint.json feedback.filesActuallyTouched, but the actual src/test/deploy file changes only land in working tree (never staged). Sprint-close merge brings empty story commits to main; PM catches it via post-merge git status; rescue commit recovers the work. PM-side detection cost is high (only caught by accident at sprint close). This story extends sprint_lint.py with a commit-vs-claim verifier: for each story with feedback.filesActuallyTouched populated, walk git log between sprint-base and HEAD on the sprint branch + verify each claimed file appears in at least one commit's tree-diff. Emit error if claimed-but-missing. Run before sprint-close merge. Closes AI-002.
